descripcion:
0DTE options expire the same day, which makes them extremely sensitive to small moves in the S&P.

When enough traders crowd into the same strikes, market makers have to hedge with futures.

At enough size, that hedge can push the index higher, lower, or pin it near a level.

The option may cost pennies.

The hedge behind it can move billions.

Comment LETTER and I’ll send you the Crown Macro Letter.
transcripcion: Same day baby options worth pennies are controlling the entire stock market. Let me explain. In 2022, the CBOE released options that expire the same day and they called them zero DTE. So if you buy a zero DTE call option on the S &P 500, you're not just betting that the market's going to go up. You're betting that it goes up enough, fast enough before the closing bell. And that's why these options can trade for pennies because there is little time value embedded in the option. but that doesn't mean that these little guys aren't powerful. No one, not even the CBOE could have predicted this, but this product exploded. According to CBOE's own research, zero DTE options reached 62 .4 % of total SPX options volume in 2025. So zero DTE aren't some little tiny casino in the corner of the market. This is now becoming the market. And once something new like this becomes so large, I mean, the market. And once something new like this becomes so large, it can start changing price behavior. And here's how it works. When traders buy same -day calls, guess what? A market maker is selling the calls, but the market makers aren't just gonna sit there sitting naked short to upside. So they hedge the position with SPX futures. Let's just say the market starts rallying. Those zero DTE calls start becoming more sensitive because they expire today. And that sensitivity is called gamma. So in this case, the dealer might have to buy more futures, which can force... So in this case, the dealer might have to buy more futures, which can force other hedgers to buy more futures, which pushes SPX higher, which can force more hedging and can force the market higher again. As you can see, this tiny little option is like the first domino in a huge chain reaction. And famously, the zero DTE options do two things really well. Number one, they can pin the market near a level, creating sticky price action on round handles like 7 ,400. And number two, they can accelerate the market away from a level. 7 ,400. And number two, it can accelerate the market away from a level. And this is why professionals like myself watch the DTE options flow. Because this flow, while it may sound insignificant, can give you a sense of where price is headed early. The first is understanding what condition the market is in, meaning what direction are you seeing the market moving towards and do you expect volatility. Now, there are two easy ways to do this. The first of which is understanding where we are at relative. two easy ways to do this the first of which is understanding where we are at relative to the volume profile from the previous day if the market is under yesterday's value area lean bearish if we are above it lean bullish but that's just one part of condition you need to understand volatility meaning how fast the market is likely to move so the way we're going to do this is twofold one we're going to look into areas of either low or high volume if we're trading into a low volume area expect fast price action and if we're trading into high expect choppiness the second is going to be understanding gamma. Now, gamma is essentially the positioning of options dealers in the market. And if you are in a negative gamma environment, then you need to understand that the market is likely going to move very quickly and breakouts are favored. And if you're in a positive gamma environment, then you're likely to see failed breakouts and more reversal strategies favored. Now, I post gamma reports every single day to my free community. And the third is dealer gamma. If you master these three, you can literally operate like a hedge fund. So first is the CFTC COT I just showed you on my screen right there. Number two is prime brokerage data. Goldman Sachs prime brokerage data is like the holy grail. They track every hedge fund book they finance, and this is gross exposure, net exposure, sector tilts, and even factor names. This stuff is granular, and it's not some cheesy survey they email to hedge fund managers. It's the actual data. These notes go out to institutional clients, and it occasionally gets leaked. If you have this in your hand, you can spot short squeezes from a mile away. Number three is dealer gamma. You have to keep in mind, options dealers have the other side of every single institutional and retail options trade. So when dealers are short gamma, moves can get amplified. And when dealers are long gamma, moves are suppressed, and the gamma can act like a magnet. Positive gamma levels are the actual support and resistance in the market. 0DTE options expire the same day, which makes them extremely sensitive to small moves in the S&P.

When enough traders crowd into the same strikes, market makers have to hedge with futures.

At enough size, that hedge can push the index higher, lower, or pin it near a level.

The option may cost pennies.

The hedge behind it can move billions.

transcripcion: Same day baby options worth pennies are controlling the entire stock market. Let me explain. In 2022, the CBOE released options that expire the same day and they called them zero DTE. So if you buy a zero DTE call option on the S &P 500, you're not just betting that the market's going to go up. You're betting that it goes up enough, fast enough before the closing bell. And that's why these options can trade for pennies because there is little time value embedded in the option. but that doesn't mean that these little guys aren't powerful. No one, not even the CBOE could have predicted this, but this product exploded. According to CBOE's own research, zero DTE options reached 62 .4 % of total SPX options volume in 2025. So zero DTE aren't some little tiny casino in the corner of the market. This is now becoming the market. And once something new like this becomes so large, I mean, the market. And once something new like this becomes so large, it can start changing price behavior. And here's how it works. When traders buy same -day calls, guess what? A market maker is selling the calls, but the market makers aren't just gonna sit there sitting naked short to upside. So they hedge the position with SPX futures. Let's just say the market starts rallying. Those zero DTE calls start becoming more sensitive because they expire today. And that sensitivity is called gamma. So in this case, the dealer might have to buy more futures, which can force... So in this case, the dealer might have to buy more futures, which can force other hedgers to buy more futures, which pushes SPX higher, which can force more hedging and can force the market higher again. As you can see, this tiny little option is like the first domino in a huge chain reaction. And famously, the zero DTE options do two things really well. Number one, they can pin the market near a level, creating sticky price action on round handles like 7 ,400. And number two, they can accelerate the market away from a level. 7 ,400. Sure, every trader understands risk management, but few traders know how to actually use it. So I'm going to give you a full step -by -step plan on how to do this. comes down to three key elements. The first is understanding what condition the market is in, meaning what direction are you seeing the market moving towards and do you expect volatility. Now, there are two easy ways to do this. The first of which is understanding where we are at relative. two easy ways to do this the first of which is understanding where we are at relative to the volume profile from the previous day if the market is under yesterday's value area lean bearish if we are above it lean bullish but that's just one part of condition you need to understand volatility meaning how fast the market is likely to move so the way we're going to do this is twofold one we're going to look into areas of either low or high volume if we're trading into a low volume area expect fast price action and if we're trading into high expect choppiness the second is going to be understanding gamma. Now, gamma is essentially the positioning of options dealers in the market. And if you are in a negative gamma environment, then you need to understand that the market is likely going to move very quickly and breakouts are favored. And if you're in a positive gamma environment, then you're likely to see failed breakouts and more reversal strategies favored. Now, I post gamma reports every single day to my free community. If you comment the word join, I'll DM you an invite. So that covers condition, but the word join, I'll DM you an invite. So that covers condition. But next, what we need to talk about is understanding what your A plus setup is. If you are in a time where the market is a little bit less likely in your favor, then risk less. You also need to understand in which conditions do you do well. So this takes some time and understanding of yourself. For me, I know that I do very well in gamma negative environments. And then also when we're trading into low volume and you're probably thinking, oh, that sounds like a lot. transcripcion: I wish I knew this when I just started trading, but price is a lagging indicator and it's positioning data that really matters. Let me explain. And it's not just one source, it's three sources of positioning data that traders use to get ahead of every move. And if you master these three, you can literally operate like a hedge fund. The first is the CFTC COT, and that stands for the commitment of traders. I'll drop a screenshot right here so you can see it. The COT is published every single Friday at 3 .30 PM. It's free and it's public and most importantly it covers 20 trillion dollars in futures and this is everything from agricultural commodities to nat gas to crude oil metals and most importantly the e -mini s p future contract the cftc cot does a really great job of telling you when a position is getting crowded or still has room to run number two is prime brokerage data goldman sachs prime brokerage data is like the holy grail here goldman tracks Prime brokerage data is like the holy grail. Here, Goldman tracks every hedge fund book they finance, and this covers gross exposure, net exposure, and even sector tilts. So this thing gets pretty granular. And this isn't just some cheesy survey they send to hedge fund managers. This is the actual data. And this data goes out to Goldman's institutional clients, and it occasionally gets leaked. If you have several banks' prime brokerage data in your hands, you can spot a short squeeze from a mile away. Number three is dealer gamma and this is the most important real -time data you have to keep in mind options dealers have the other side of every single institutional and retail trade so when dealers are short gamma moves can get well exaggerated as dealers often join a rally or keep selling into a falling market and when dealers are long gamma moves are suppressed and gamma buildup can act like a magnet as dealers buy dips and sell rallies. Positive gamma levels are the actual support and resistance in the market, not hand -drawn trend lines. Now, in order for all this to be useful, you have to compile all this data together. In isolation, these data points can be noise. Now, the CFTC data, you can pull that yourself, but for everything else, I roll it into the crown macro letter so you have a composite picture of positioning.
